Class TransactWriteBuilder

java.lang.Object
com.hogwai.dynamodb.simplified.builder.TransactWriteBuilder
public class TransactWriteBuilder extends Object
Builds a transactional write operation that groups up to 100 put, update, delete, and condition check actions atomically across one or more tables.

Obtain via DynamoSimplifiedClient.transactWrite().

All actions succeed or none are applied. If any condition fails or a conflict occurs, the entire transaction is rejected.

  • Method Details

    • put

      public <T> @NonNull TransactWriteBuilder put(@NonNull Table<T> table, @NonNull T item)
      Adds a put action to insert or replace an item.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the item type
      Parameters:
      table - the typed table to write to
      item - the item to put
      Returns:
      this builder

    • update

      public <T> @NonNull TransactWriteBuilder update(@NonNull Table<T> table, @NonNull T item)
      Adds an update action to modify an existing item.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the item type
      Parameters:
      table - the typed table to update
      item - the item with updated values
      Returns:
      this builder

    • update

      public <T> @NonNull TransactWriteBuilder update(@NonNull Table<T> table, @NonNull T item, @NonNull Consumer<UpdateExpression> expressionConfigurator)
      Adds an update action with a partial update expression for fine-grained attribute modifications.

      Use the expressionConfigurator to specify SET, REMOVE, ADD, and/or DELETE clauses. When an expression-based update is included in the transaction, the entire transaction is sent via the low-level DynamoDB API rather than the enhanced client.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the item type
      Parameters:
      table - the typed table to update
      item - the item providing the key identifying the item to update
      expressionConfigurator - a consumer to build the update expression
      Returns:
      this builder

    • delete

      public <T> @NonNull TransactWriteBuilder delete(@NonNull Table<T> table, @NonNull Object partitionKey)
      Adds a delete action by partition key.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the item type
      Parameters:
      table - the typed table to delete from
      partitionKey - the partition key value
      Returns:
      this builder

    • delete

      public <T> @NonNull TransactWriteBuilder delete(@NonNull Table<T> table, @NonNull Object partitionKey, @NonNull Object sortKey)
      Adds a delete action by partition and sort key.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the item type
      Parameters:
      table - the typed table to delete from
      partitionKey - the partition key value
      sortKey - the sort key value
      Returns:
      this builder

    • conditionCheck

      public <T> @NonNull TransactWriteBuilder conditionCheck(@NonNull Table<T> table, @NonNull Object partitionKey, @NonNull Consumer<ConditionExpression.Builder> condition)
      Adds a condition check action that verifies a condition on an item without modifying it.

      If the condition is not satisfied, the entire transaction is rejected.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the item type
      Parameters:
      table - the typed table to check
      partitionKey - the partition key value
      condition - a consumer to build the condition expression (e.g. expr -> expr.eq("status", "active"))
      Returns:
      this builder

    • conditionCheck

      public <T> @NonNull TransactWriteBuilder conditionCheck(@NonNull Table<T> table, @NonNull Object partitionKey, @Nullable Object sortKey, @NonNull Consumer<ConditionExpression.Builder> condition)
      Adds a condition check action with sort key.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the item type
      Parameters:
      table - the typed table to check
      partitionKey - the partition key value
      sortKey - the sort key value
      condition - a consumer to build the condition expression
      Returns:
      this builder

    • execute

      public void execute()
      Executes the transactional write operation.

      All actions are applied atomically. If any action fails, a TransactionCanceledException is thrown.

      If any operation in the transaction uses an UpdateExpression, the entire transaction is sent via the low-level DynamoDB API. Otherwise, the enhanced DynamoDB client is used.
